fertilizers.name: Urea
nutrients: Little to no phosphorus or potassium
uses: Nitrogen-focused correction when crops need leaf and stem growth support
grading_notes: Compare against recommendations that primarily address nitrogen
Weak overlap with fertilizers selected for phosphorus or potassium support

fertilizers.name: DAP
uses: Phosphorus support for root development, early growth, flowering, or seed formation while adding some nitrogen
grading_notes: Meaningful overlap with recommendations that supply nitrogen and phosphorus
Weak overlap with potassium-containing blends when potassium is central

uses: Strong phosphorus support when some nitrogen and potassium are also useful
grading_notes: Overlaps with phosphorus-heavy fertilizers and mixed N-P-K blends
Stronger match when the explanation calls for phosphorus plus some potassium

nutrients: Little or no potassium in this dataset context
uses: Higher nitrogen-phosphorus support when potassium is not the main concern
grading_notes: Overlaps with two-nutrient N-P fertilizers such as 20-20
Weaker match when potassium support is needed

uses: General-purpose balanced support across growth, root development, flowering, and plant strength
grading_notes: Best match when all three major nutrients are relevant rather than one nutrient dominating

nutrients: Little or no potassium in this dataset context
uses: Moderate nitrogen-phosphorus support when potassium is not the priority
grading_notes: Overlaps with 28-28, but is less concentrated
Weaker match for cases requiring potassium

uses: Root, flowering, fruiting, or plant-strength support where phosphorus and potassium matter more than nitrogen
grading_notes: Overlaps with mixed N-P-K fertilizers and P-K-forward reasoning
Weak match when nitrogen-only support is the central need
